body.title: [sprint] Intent extractor: auto-populate intention field from ticket + code context
body.intention: I intend that the intention field on tickets is populated by inference from existing ticket prose + affected file symbols, not by manual authoring.
body.description:
We have IBD: every ticket has an `intention:` field (I intend that...), manually authored at filing time. An intent extractor agent would infer this from the ticket description, affected files, and symbol map — making it a compiled artifact rather than a hand-filled field.

Use cases:
- Backfill `intention:` on tickets filed without one
- Validate that the manually-authored intention matches what the code actually does post-sprint
- Feed into scenario generator (intent → scenarios)

Observed in working form at a parallel project (test automation context, serves non-engineer test authors).

**Affected files:** TBD — new inference agent; likely lab/claudecode/ or devices/hubert/
**Design rules:** none — inference agent, no DB writes required beyond updating the ticket field
**Scope boundary:** IN — infer intention from prose + symbols. OUT — acting on the intention (that's scenario-generator).
**Completion criteria:** Intent extractor produces a plausible `intention:` sentence for 5 sample tickets; at least 3 match the manually-authored original within one Akien review.
